kasper schema defines min_observations_for_update as a bounded integer floor
kasper applies the minimum observation floor before improvement side effects
loop-cli-main parses maxRuns as an explicit cap and tests stop at maxRuns

minimum floor is a behavioral invariant that range-only search cannot protect
add invariant groups before broadening optimizer products to context/council

title: Define one ADR-owned antiConvergence contract and project it into all graph-backed loop configs
why: S4-02 covered research-only minIterations; S6-04 needs one stop-policy contract projected into all graph-backed mode configs.

title: Keep convergenceMode outside optimizer tunables and gate minIterations behind paired constraints
why: Numeric floors can be bounded; semantic stop-mode switches must stay locked, and minIterations cannot be searched until minIterations <= maxIterations is enforceable.

title: Project the contract into council as minRounds without renaming council-native fields
why: Council uses rounds and agreement, so the ADR should map shared anti-convergence semantics into council vocabulary rather than forcing research-style field names.

title: Add stop-policy capability metadata to runtime_capabilities.json and validate it fail-closed
why: Runtime capability metadata currently covers tools/mirrors, not whether the runtime/mode honors anti-convergence floors.

title: Extend optimizer manifest/search/promote from independent ranges to invariant groups
why: Range-only optimizer search can satisfy each numeric bound while violating cross-field stop-policy invariants.

text: S4-02 and S4-03 already covered research-only minIterations, convergenceMode, optimizer treatment, and YAML stop-order placement. This iteration adds cross-mode config projection, council vocabulary mapping, and runtime capability validation.

deep-context has maxIterations/convergenceThreshold/stuckThreshold but no _optimizerManaged block and no runtime_capabilities.json peer found in the searched assets, so the ADR must decide whether to add context-specific capability metadata or centralize graph-backed mode capability shape.
